item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me after one SCP-2661

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image comes from here and is copyrighted, the second image comes from here and is copyrighted, and the two other images cannot be conclusively sourced. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I first thought the minotaur was there just for the WTF-factor. Only realized the maze connection after reading the comments, and man did that make the article better. Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I've been confusing Figure and Ground" is an excellent metaphor for that moment when you realize the intricate geometric patterns are also maze designs, and the thing about offering oneself for reverse communion is also an approximation of sending the virgins into the labyrinth.

There's just the one twist between the beginning and the end… but it's a damn good twist, executed well, and the supplemental materials add detail to the story and the phenomenon without being an Accompanying Note From The Creator Which Explains Everything. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BUT WHERE'S KOZILEK?!??!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Once I finished reading this I actually said, out loud, "the Labyrinth. Fuck, it's the Labyrinth." I feel like I'm a member of a Foundation response team now LOL

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this. I thought it was a good "predatory otherwordly entity" story. I didn't pick up on the whole "maze and minotaur" idea until I saw some of the comments mentioning it. Huh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The minotaur bit didn't click for me until the very end, and it didn't sit well. It was interesting initially in that it made little to no sense, but then I went "Oh, they're making labyrinths for (a) minotaur(s)."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When bismuth is melted and cooled, it solidifies into those unusual crystal shapes. So you don't have to carve them into that desired shape.

In fact, here's a gift shop two hours from where I live that sells a bunch of bismuth 'rocks', each with that distinctive patterns, for about a dollar each.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: could you elaborate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somebody must be pretty obsessed with the pattern to take the time to carve out a piece of bismuth in that shape instead of just simply growing one on a household stove.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And who is to say it doesn't?
Maybe this drug WAS around in that time for a bit, and that's where the Minotaur idea came from in our world.

So, it can -still- be an alien deity, just we happen to know it from a different source and consider it a myth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. I had machine elves in mind originally. It would be horrible wouldn't it. Thanks for taking an interest =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beyond the interesting pictures, and very well-written diary, it's just a thing that crazies you to death - and that thing is basically just addictive DMT (in fact, so similar, that it was unsettling. The nice thing about DMT is that it fades so quickly. Imagining it persisting is quite horrifying).

Yes, there's the bovine entity, but evidence for its existence is very circumspect.

Basically, I don't really see why this is contained. It is pretty well-written, though. Hmm. I'm hovering, but I think no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I've decided I don't like it in the article. I may spin it off as a tale or something.
Basically, I like the article to be about as subtle/vague as it stands now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any particular reason the diary was removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked before, and I like it now. The new image you added was an improvement too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still really like this one, though it have the same problem with it I had before, that being the shoehorned Minotaur. If it was anything else I'd be happy. I rather like the idea of a drug that makes its users summon an alien deity, but I'm less fond of one that make its users make a Grecian maze for a bull man. Still +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really good, the diary is great.

700 acres is an insane number though. That's over a square mile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The format bugged me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's even better now.

+1 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i liked it then and i like it now but i can't say that i'm a fan of the "hoofmaiden" thing. it comes off as far too cutesy and cheesy for an article like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was unhappy with it so I shelved/deleted it. I thought I'd return to it a couple weeks from now. Turns I had some time so I made the diary the emphasis and posted that version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I already liked it before, and the diary is a good addition, so happy to upvote. Why did you take down the first version if I might ask?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my revision of an earlier SCP i deleted. It's substantially changed.
Im getting the images now.

—edit

I'm having trouble getting the images to work. Still trying.

—edit
If anyone knows how to get the images to work, please go ahead and edit the source….

—edit
Fixed it finally. I'm sorry about that.
